action.reasoning: Observation: Baseline MetricGAN+ gives PESQ 3.134. All simple post-processing tricks failed. Generator has 1.9M params - small enough to fine-tune. Analysis: Best path forward is to fine-tune the MetricGAN+ generator on the training data using a PESQ-aware loss. Since we don't have time to set up the full adversarial PESQ-surrogate (discriminator) training, I'll try a simpler approach: fine-tune with magnitude L1 loss on training pairs - but this often hurts PESQ. Better: use a PESQ-objective optimizer like directly maximizing PESQ via a soft surrogate. Actually, the cleanest approach: implement MetricGAN+'s actual training procedure (generator + PESQ-predicting discriminator), or use the available auraloss MultiResolutionSTFTLoss as proxy. Let me first explore the generator architecture more carefully then implement a quick fine-tune. Decision: Inspect model structure, then write a fine-tuning script.
